subject: Make the pnf registration test cases independent from robot init - fixing failing test case in xtesting
commit_message:
Make the pnf registration test cases independent from robot init - fixing failing test case in xtesting

Issue-ID: INT-1407

Signed-off-by: Krzysztof Kuzmicki <krzysztof.kuzmicki@nokia.com>
Change-Id: Ic06d5f525a9422f9b67c8e30eca8d0759ab20bf0

entries.message: Patch Set 2: Commit message was updated.

Patch Set 2:

isnt the whole point of init to do this setup?

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> isnt the whole point of init to do this setup?

Xtesting is not doing init during test executions (takes a lot of time). PNF requires to have some dummy region because it is not instantiating any VNF. As in Xtesting I don't know if Morgan is using valid openstack.yaml file due to this I cannot use native init mechanism because it gets tennat id directly from openstack. It requires connection to OpenStack. Also native init mechanism creates at one shot both region and tenant. 
If I by mistake would create RegionOne with some dummy tenant and next execute init it will fail. I don't want to break init.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > Patch Set 2:
> > 
> > isnt the whole point of init to do this setup?
> 
> Xtesting is not doing init during test executions (takes a lot of time). PNF requires to have some dummy region because it is not instantiating any VNF. As in Xtesting I don't know if Morgan is using valid openstack.yaml file due to this I cannot use native init mechanism because it gets tennat id directly from openstack. It requires connection to OpenStack. Also native init mechanism creates at one shot both region and tenant. 
> If I by mistake would create RegionOne with some dummy tenant and next execute init it will fail. I don't want to break init.
The problem is in 

Initialize Customer And Models
    [Tags]   InitDemo
    Load Customer And Models   Demonstration

Load Customer
    [Documentation]   Use ONAP to Orchestrate a service.
    [Arguments]    ${customer_name}
    ${tenant_id}    ${tenant_name}=    Setup Orchestrate VNF   ${GLOBAL_AAI_CLOUD_OWNER}   SharedNode    OwnerType    v1    CloudZone

tenant_id and tenant_name is not taken from override but directly from openstack during init.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> isnt the whole point of init to do this setup?

init is doing a big init for all the use cases based on config set in the override.yaml file used by the robot pod during installation

for the use cases in CI, we need to keep the time under control and cannot perform a global init, which is long and setup lots of things we do not need.
And when like here no OpenStack connectivity is really required, it can speed up the test and focus on the purpose of the tests.
such evolution may be needed for the tests integrated in CI but should not break anything when dealing with the init part
let's give a try
Change has been successfully merged by Morgan Richomme

Patch Set 2:

Build Successful
